applied_fixes: BookImportDialog.tsx:268 — added silent-catch-ok marker for non-fatal EPUB chapter extraction failure
LinkFormatsDialog.tsx:344 — added silent-catch-ok marker for nested rollback catch (outer toast already covers user feedback)

findings.title: Hardcoded 0.7 confidence floor duplicates INTRA_CHAPTER_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR constant
findings.why_it_matters: Two sources of truth for the same threshold. If the resolver constant ever changes (Story B.5 explicitly calls this out), the hook's audio→EPUB path will silently diverge.
findings.suggestedFix: Import INTRA_CHAPTER_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR from '@/lib/chapterSwitchResolver' and replace 0.7 with the constant.

findings.title: handleBookmarkDeleted useCallback missing onBookmarkChange dependency
findings.why_it_matters: Pre-existing dep-array bug, but the new BookReader integration (E103) wires onBookmarkChange to refresh the bookmark icon — a stale closure here will skip the refresh after the prop identity changes. handleBookmarkCreated above correctly lists [onBookmarkChange].
evidence: AudiobookRenderer.tsx:169-176 — uses onBookmarkChange?.() but deps are []

findings.title: Defensive bug: findIndex(-1)+1 = 0 selects first chapter as 'next' on lookup miss
findings.why_it_matters: When the target chapter is not found in the sorted list (sorted.findIndex returns -1), `(-1 + 1) | 0` evaluates to 0, so endPct is computed against chapters[0] instead of bailing. Produces wrong chapterPct → wrong CFI on a degenerate book. The `| 0` is also dead code (the expression is already an integer).
findings.suggestedFix: Compute idx separately: `const idx = sorted.findIndex(c => c.id === chapter.id); const nextCh = idx >= 0 ? sorted[idx + 1] : undefined;` then bail when undefined.

findings.title: resolveTargetChapterIndex assumes mapping array is densely keyed by EPUB index
findings.why_it_matters: EPUB→Audio uses `mapping.mappings[currentChapterIndex]` (array index = EPUB chapter index). Audio→EPUB returns `mapping.mappings.indexOf(entry)` as the EPUB target chapter index. Both rely on the array being sorted/dense by EPUB chapter index with no gaps. If chapterMatcher ever drops chapters or reorders, the chapter-start fallback path will jump to the wrong chapter. Worth an explicit comment in ChapterMappingRecord type or a lookup helper.
evidence: useFormatSwitch.ts:75 indexOf(entry) used as chapter index
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) document the invariant on ChapterMappingRecord, or (b) store epubChapterIndex on each ChapterMapping entry and look up by field rather than array index.

findings.title: `void db` smell hides unused import
findings.why_it_matters: `db` is imported but only referenced via `void db` to silence an unused warning. The actual write goes through syncableWrite; the import is dead.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove the `import { db } from '@/db/schema'` line and the trailing `void db`.

findings.title: Format-switch URL-strip useEffect has empty deps but reads searchParams/navigate
findings.why_it_matters: Empty deps `[]` is intentional (run-once on mount), but values like searchParams and location.pathname are captured at first render. If the component is mounted with no params and then params arrive via navigation without remount, the strip never runs. In practice the component remounts on route change because of the keyed render, so impact is low — but worth an `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comment with a justification.
evidence: BookReader.tsx:664 `}, []) // Run once on mount only`
findings.suggestedFix: Add explicit eslint-disable comment with justification, or capture initial params via refs and run effect on every render guarded by the refs.

findings.title: setTimeout(seekTo, 100) on initial chapter seek has no cleanup
findings.why_it_matters: If the renderer unmounts within 100ms of mount (rapid back-nav from format switch), the deferred seek fires on a stale audio element. Pattern matches the existing handleBookmarkSeek so consistent, but neither has cleanup. Low real-world impact; consider tracking with a ref + cleanup for robustness.
findings.suggestedFix: Capture timer id, clear in effect cleanup.

findings.title: switchingRef never resets on early returns; relies on unmount
findings.why_it_matters: switchingRef.current = true is set before navigate(); the comment notes the component unmounts on navigation. If navigation is preempted (e.g., guard intercepts, user hits back rapidly), the guard latches and subsequent format switches are silently no-ops until remount. Low likelihood; consider try/finally with reset after a short timeout.
findings.suggestedFix: Reset switchingRef in a setTimeout(50) after navigate, or in a useEffect cleanup tied to location.

findings.title: resetTimerRef has no unmount cleanup
findings.why_it_matters: If the dialog unmounts during the 300ms reset delay, the setTimeout fires setView on an unmounted component. React 18+ swallows this silently but a useEffect cleanup is the conventional fix.

story_a_units_addressed: A.3 lazy ABS chapter fetch in LinkFormatsDialog.handlePairPressed
A.4 persist mapping with rollback on failure
story_b_units_addressed: B.3 receivers honor URL params (BookReader, AudiobookRenderer)
B.5 confidence floor (constant exported, used in resolver; F1: hardcoded duplicate in hook)
